verses.bsb: For I was ashamed to ask the king for an escort of soldiers and horsemen to protect us from our enemies on the road, since we had told him, “The hand of our God is gracious to all who seek Him, but His great anger is against all who forsake Him.”

verses.esv: So we fasted and implored our God for this, and he listened to our entreaty.
verses.kjv: So we fasted and besought our God for this: and he was intreated of us.
verses.bsb: So we fasted and petitioned our God about this, and He granted our request.

verses.esv: Then I set apart twelve of the leading priests: Sherebiah, Hashabiah, and ten of their kinsmen with them.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then I separated twelve of the chief of the priests, Sherebiah, Hashabiah, and ten of their brethren with them,
verses.bsb: Then I set apart twelve of the leading priests, together with Sherebiah, Hashabiah, and ten of their brothers,

verses.esv: And I weighed out to them the silver and the gold and the vessels, the offering for the house of our God that the king and his counselors and his lords and all Israel there present had offered.
verses.kjv: And weighed unto them the silver, and the gold, and the vessels, even the offering of the house of our God, which the king, and his counsellers, and his lords, and all Israel there present, had offered:
verses.bsb: and I weighed out to them the contribution of silver and gold and the articles that the king, his counselors, his leaders, and all the Israelites there had offered for the house of our God.

verses.esv: I weighed out into their hand 650 talents of silver, and silver vessels worth 200 talents, and 100 talents of gold,
verses.kjv: I even weighed unto their hand six hundred and fifty talents of silver, and silver vessels an hundred talents, and of gold an hundred talents;
verses.bsb: I weighed out into their hands 650 talents of silver, articles of silver weighing 100 talents, 100 talents of gold,

verses.esv: 20 bowls of gold worth 1,000 darics, and two vessels of fine bright bronze as precious as gold.
verses.kjv: Also twenty basons of gold, of a thousand drams; and two vessels of fine copper, precious as gold.
verses.bsb: 20 gold bowls valued at 1,000 darics, and two articles of fine polished bronze, as precious as gold.

verses.esv: And I said to them, “You are holy to the LORD, and the vessels are holy, and the silver and the gold are a freewill offering to the LORD, the God of your fathers.
verses.kjv: And I said unto them, Ye are holy unto the Lord; the vessels are holy also; and the silver and the gold are a freewill offering unto the Lord God of your fathers.
verses.bsb: Then I told them, “You are holy to the LORD, and these articles are holy. The silver and gold are a freewill offering to the LORD, the God of your fathers.

verses.esv: Guard them and keep them until you weigh them before the chief priests and the Levites and the heads of fathers' houses in Israel at Jerusalem, within the chambers of the house of the LORD.”
verses.kjv: Watch ye, and keep them, until ye weigh them before the chief of the priests and the Levites, and chief of the fathers of Israel, at Jerusalem, in the chambers of the house of the Lord.
verses.bsb: Guard them carefully until you weigh them out in the chambers of the house of the LORD in Jerusalem before the leading priests, Levites, and heads of the Israelite families.”

verses.esv: So the priests and the Levites took over the weight of the silver and the gold and the vessels, to bring them to Jerusalem, to the house of our God.
verses.kjv: So took the priests and the Levites the weight of the silver, and the gold, and the vessels, to bring them to Jerusalem unto the house of our God.
verses.bsb: So the priests and Levites took charge of the silver and gold and sacred articles that had been weighed out to be taken to the house of our God in Jerusalem.

verses.esv: Then we departed from the river Ahava on the twelfth day of the first month, to go to Jerusalem. The hand of our God was on us, and he delivered us from the hand of the enemy and from ambushes by the way.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then we departed from the river of Ahava on the twelfth day of the first month, to go unto Jerusalem: and the hand of our God was upon us, and he delivered us from the hand of the enemy, and of such as lay in wait by the way.
verses.bsb: On the twelfth day of the first month we set out from the Ahava Canal to go to Jerusalem, and the hand of our God was upon us to protect us from the hands of the enemies and bandits along the way.

verses.esv: We came to Jerusalem, and there we remained three days.
verses.kjv: And we came to Jerusalem, and abode there three days.
verses.bsb: So we arrived at Jerusalem and rested there for three days.

verses.esv: On the fourth day, within the house of our God, the silver and the gold and the vessels were weighed into the hands of Meremoth the priest, son of Uriah, and with him was Eleazar the son of Phinehas, and with them were the Levites, Jozabad the son of Jeshua and Noadiah the son of Binnui.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now on the fourth day was the silver and the gold and the vessels weighed in the house of our God by the hand of Meremoth the son of Uriah the priest; and with him was Eleazar the son of Phinehas; and with them was Jozabad the son of Jeshua, and Noadiah the son of Binnui, Levites;
verses.bsb: On the fourth day, in the house of our God, we weighed out the silver and gold and sacred articles into the hand of Meremoth son of Uriah, the priest. Eleazar son of Phinehas was with him, along with the Levites Jozabad son of Jeshua and Noadiah son of Binnui.

verses.esv: The whole was counted and weighed, and the weight of everything was recorded.
verses.kjv: By number and by weight of every one: and all the weight was written at that time.
verses.bsb: Everything was verified by number and weight, and the total weight was recorded at that time.

verses.esv: At that time those who had come from captivity, the returned exiles, offered burnt offerings to the God of Israel, twelve bulls for all Israel, ninety-six rams, seventy-seven lambs, and as a sin offering twelve male goats. All this was a burnt offering to the LORD.
verses.kjv: Also the children of those that had been carried away, which were come out of the captivity, offered burnt offerings unto the God of Israel, twelve bullocks for all Israel, ninety and six rams, seventy and seven lambs, twelve he goats for a sin offering: all this was a burnt offering unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Then the exiles who had returned from captivity sacrificed burnt offerings to the God of Israel: 12 bulls for all Israel, 96 rams, 77 lambs, and a sin offering of 12 male goats. All this was a burnt offering to the LORD.

verses.esv: They also delivered the king's commissions to the king's satraps and to the governors of the province Beyond the River, and they aided the people and the house of God.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they delivered the king’s commissions unto the king’s lieutenants, and to the governors on this side the river: and they furthered the people, and the house of God.
verses.bsb: They also delivered the king’s edicts to the royal satraps and governors of the region west of the Euphrates, who proceeded to assist the people and the house of God.

verses.esv: After these things had been done, the officials approached me and said, “The people of Israel and the priests and the Levites have not separated themselves from the peoples of the lands with their abominations, from the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Jebusites, the Ammonites, the Moabites, the Egyptians, and the Amorites.
verses.kjv: NOW when these things were done, the princes came to me, saying, The people of Israel, and the priests, and the Levites, have not separated themselves from the people of the lands, doing according to their abominations, even of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Jebusites, the Ammonites, the Moabites, the Egyptians, and the Amorites.
verses.bsb: After these things had been accomplished, the leaders approached me and said, “The people of Israel, including the priests and Levites, have not kept themselves separate from the surrounding peoples whose abominations are like those of the Canaanites, Hittites, Perizzites, Jebusites, Ammonites, Moabites, Egyptians, and Amorites.

verses.esv: For they have taken some of their daughters to be wives for themselves and for their sons, so that the holy race has mixed itself with the peoples of the lands. And in this faithlessness the hand of the officials and chief men has been foremost.”
verses.kjv: For they have taken of their daughters for themselves, and for their sons: so that the holy seed have mingled themselves with the people of those lands: yea, the hand of the princes and rulers hath been chief in this trespass.
verses.bsb: Indeed, the Israelites have taken some of their daughters as wives for themselves and their sons, so that the holy seed has been mixed with the people of the land. And the leaders and officials have taken the lead in this unfaithfulness!”

verses.esv: As soon as I heard this, I tore my garment and my cloak and pulled hair from my head and beard and sat appalled.
verses.kjv: And when I heard this thing, I rent my garment and my mantle, and plucked off the hair of my head and of my beard, and sat down astonied.
verses.bsb: When I heard this report, I tore my tunic and cloak, pulled out some hair from my head and beard, and sat down in horror.

verses.esv: Then all who trembled at the words of the God of Israel, because of the faithlessness of the returned exiles, gathered around me while I sat appalled until the evening sacrifice.
verses.kjv: Then were assembled unto me every one that trembled at the words of the God of Israel, because of the transgression of those that had been carried away; and I sat astonied until the evening sacrifice.
verses.bsb: Then everyone who trembled at the words of the God of Israel gathered around me because of the unfaithfulness of the exiles, while I sat there in horror until the evening offering.

verses.esv: And at the evening sacrifice I rose from my fasting, with my garment and my cloak torn, and fell upon my knees and spread out my hands to the LORD my God,
verses.kjv: ¶ And at the evening sacrifice I arose up from my heaviness; and having rent my garment and my mantle, I fell upon my knees, and spread out my hands unto the Lord my God,
verses.bsb: At the evening offering, I got up from my humiliation with my tunic and cloak torn, and I fell on my knees, spread out my hands to the LORD my God,

verses.esv: saying: “O my God, I am ashamed and blush to lift my face to you, my God, for our iniquities have risen higher than our heads, and our guilt has mounted up to the heavens.
verses.kjv: And said, O my God, I am ashamed and blush to lift up my face to thee, my God: for our iniquities are increased over our head, and our trespass is grown up unto the heavens.
verses.bsb: and said:  “O my God, I am ashamed and embarrassed to lift up my face to You, my God, because our iniquities are higher than our heads, and our guilt has reached the heavens.

verses.esv: From the days of our fathers to this day we have been in great guilt. And for our iniquities we, our kings, and our priests have been given into the hand of the kings of the lands, to the sword, to captivity, to plundering, and to utter shame, as it is today.
verses.kjv: Since the days of our fathers have we been in a great trespass unto this day; and for our iniquities have we, our kings, and our priests, been delivered into the hand of the kings of the lands, to the sword, to captivity, and to a spoil, and to confusion of face, as it is this day.
verses.bsb: From the days of our fathers to this day, our guilt has been great. Because of our iniquities, we and our kings and priests have been delivered into the hands of the kings of the earth and put to the sword and captivity, to pillage and humiliation, as we are this day.

verses.esv: But now for a brief moment favor has been shown by the LORD our God, to leave us a remnant and to give us a secure hold within his holy place, that our God may brighten our eyes and grant us a little reviving in our slavery.
verses.kjv: And now for a little space grace hath been shewed from the Lord our God, to leave us a remnant to escape, and to give us a nail in his holy place, that our God may lighten our eyes, and give us a little reviving in our bondage.
verses.bsb: But now, for a brief moment, grace has come from the LORD our God to preserve for us a remnant and to give us a stake in His holy place. Even in our bondage, our God has given us new life and light to our eyes.

verses.esv: For we are slaves. Yet our God has not forsaken us in our slavery, but has extended to us his steadfast love before the kings of Persia, to grant us some reviving to set up the house of our God, to repair its ruins, and to give us protection in Judea and Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: For we were bondmen; yet our God hath not forsaken us in our bondage, but hath extended mercy unto us in the sight of the kings of Persia, to give us a reviving, to set up the house of our God, and to repair the desolations thereof, and to give us a wall in Judah and in Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: Though we are slaves, our God has not forsaken us in our bondage, but He has extended to us grace in the sight of the kings of Persia, giving us new life to rebuild the house of our God and repair its ruins, and giving us a wall of protection in Judah and Jerusalem.

verses.esv: “And now, O our God, what shall we say after this? For we have forsaken your commandments,
verses.kjv: And now, O our God, what shall we say after this? for we have forsaken thy commandments,
verses.bsb: And now, our God, what can we say after this? For we have forsaken the commandments

verses.esv: which you commanded by your servants the prophets, saying, ‘The land that you are entering, to take possession of it, is a land impure with the impurity of the peoples of the lands, with their abominations that have filled it from end to end with their uncleanness.
verses.kjv: Which thou hast commanded by thy servants the prophets, saying, The land, unto which ye go to possess it, is an unclean land with the filthiness of the people of the lands, with their abominations, which have filled it from one end to another with their uncleanness.
verses.bsb: that You gave through Your servants the prophets, saying: ‘The land that you are entering to possess is a land polluted by the impurity of its peoples and the abominations with which they have filled it from end to end.

verses.esv: Therefore do not give your daughters to their sons, neither take their daughters for your sons, and never seek their peace or prosperity, that you may be strong and eat the good of the land and leave it for an inheritance to your children forever.’
verses.kjv: Now therefore give not your daughters unto their sons, neither take their daughters unto your sons, nor seek their peace or their wealth for ever: that ye may be strong, and eat the good of the land, and leave it for an inheritance to your children for ever.
verses.bsb: Now, therefore, do not give your daughters in marriage to their sons or take their daughters for your sons. Never seek their peace or prosperity, so that you may be strong and may eat the good things of the land, leaving it as an inheritance to your sons forever.’

verses.esv: And after all that has come upon us for our evil deeds and for our great guilt, seeing that you, our God, have punished us less than our iniquities deserved and have given us such a remnant as this,
verses.kjv: And after all that is come upon us for our evil deeds, and for our great trespass, seeing that thou our God hast punished us less than our iniquities deserve, and hast given us such deliverance as this;
verses.bsb: After all that has come upon us because of our evil deeds and our great guilt (though You, our God, have punished us less than our iniquities deserve and have given us such a remnant as this),

verses.esv: shall we break your commandments again and intermarry with the peoples who practice these abominations? Would you not be angry with us until you consumed us, so that there should be no remnant, nor any to escape?
verses.kjv: Should we again break thy commandments, and join in affinity with the people of these abominations? wouldest not thou be angry with us till thou hadst consumed us, so that there should be no remnant nor escaping?
verses.bsb: shall we again break Your commandments and intermarry with the peoples who commit these abominations? Would You not become so angry with us as to wipe us out, leaving no remnant or survivor?

verses.esv: O LORD, the God of Israel, you are just, for we are left a remnant that has escaped, as it is today. Behold, we are before you in our guilt, for none can stand before you because of this.”
verses.kjv: O Lord God of Israel, thou art righteous: for we remain yet escaped, as it is this day: behold, we are before thee in our trespasses: for we cannot stand before thee because of this.
verses.bsb: O LORD, God of Israel, You are righteous! For we remain this day as a remnant. Here we are before You in our guilt, though because of it no one can stand before You.”

verses.esv: While Ezra prayed and made confession, weeping and casting himself down before the house of God, a very great assembly of men, women, and children, gathered to him out of Israel, for the people wept bitterly.
verses.kjv: NOW when Ezra had prayed, and when he had confessed, weeping and casting himself down before the house of God, there assembled unto him out of Israel a very great congregation of men and women and children: for the people wept very sore.
verses.bsb: While Ezra prayed and made this confession, weeping and falling facedown before the house of God, a very large assembly of Israelites—men, women, and children—gathered around him, and the people wept bitterly as well.

verses.esv: And Shecaniah the son of Jehiel, of the sons of Elam, addressed Ezra: “We have broken faith with our God and have married foreign women from the peoples of the land, but even now there is hope for Israel in spite of this.
verses.kjv: And Shechaniah the son of Jehiel, one of the sons of Elam, answered and said unto Ezra, We have trespassed against our God, and have taken strange wives of the people of the land: yet now there is hope in Israel concerning this thing.
verses.bsb: Then Shecaniah son of Jehiel, an Elamite, said to Ezra: “We have been unfaithful to our God by marrying foreign women from the people of the land, yet in spite of this, there is hope for Israel.

verses.esv: Therefore let us make a covenant with our God to put away all these wives and their children, according to the counsel of my lord and of those who tremble at the commandment of our God, and let it be done according to the Law.
verses.kjv: Now therefore let us make a covenant with our God to put away all the wives, and such as are born of them, according to the counsel of my lord, and of those that tremble at the commandment of our God; and let it be done according to the law.
verses.bsb: So now let us make a covenant before our God to send away all the foreign wives and their children, according to the counsel of my lord and of those who tremble at the command of our God. Let it be done according to the Law.

verses.esv: Arise, for it is your task, and we are with you; be strong and do it.”
verses.kjv: Arise; for this matter belongeth unto thee: we also will be with thee: be of good courage, and do it.
verses.bsb: Get up, for this matter is your responsibility, and we will support you. Be strong and take action!”

verses.esv: Then Ezra arose and made the leading priests and Levites and all Israel take an oath that they would do as had been said. So they took the oath.
verses.kjv: Then arose Ezra, and made the chief priests, the Levites, and all Israel, to swear that they should do according to this word. And they sware.
verses.bsb: So Ezra got up and made the leading priests, Levites, and all Israel take an oath to do what had been said. And they took the oath.

verses.esv: Then Ezra withdrew from before the house of God and went to the chamber of Jehohanan the son of Eliashib, where he spent the night, neither eating bread nor drinking water, for he was mourning over the faithlessness of the exiles.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Ezra rose up from before the house of God, and went into the chamber of Johanan the son of Eliashib: and when he came thither, he did eat no bread, nor drink water: for he mourned because of the transgression of them that had been carried away.
verses.bsb: Then Ezra withdrew from before the house of God and walked to the chamber of Jehohanan son of Eliashib. And while he stayed there, he ate no food and drank no water, because he was mourning over the unfaithfulness of the exiles.

verses.esv: And a proclamation was made throughout Judah and Jerusalem to all the returned exiles that they should assemble at Jerusalem,
verses.kjv: And they made proclamation throughout Judah and Jerusalem unto all the children of the captivity, that they should gather themselves together unto Jerusalem;
verses.bsb: And a proclamation was issued throughout Judah and Jerusalem that all the exiles should gather at Jerusalem.

verses.esv: and that if anyone did not come within three days, by order of the officials and the elders all his property should be forfeited, and he himself banned from the congregation of the exiles.
verses.kjv: And that whosoever would not come within three days, according to the counsel of the princes and the elders, all his substance should be forfeited, and himself separated from the congregation of those that had been carried away.
verses.bsb: Whoever failed to appear within three days would forfeit all his property, according to the counsel of the leaders and elders, and would himself be expelled from the assembly of the exiles.

verses.esv: Then all the men of Judah and Benjamin assembled at Jerusalem within the three days. It was the ninth month, on the twentieth day of the month. And all the people sat in the open square before the house of God, trembling because of this matter and because of the heavy rain.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then all the men of Judah and Benjamin gathered themselves together unto Jerusalem within three days. It was the ninth month, on the twentieth day of the month; and all the people sat in the street of the house of God, trembling because of this matter, and for the great rain.
verses.bsb: So within the three days, all the men of Judah and Benjamin assembled in Jerusalem, and on the twentieth day of the ninth month, all the people sat in the square at the house of God, trembling regarding this matter and because of the heavy rain.

verses.esv: And Ezra the priest stood up and said to them, “You have broken faith and married foreign women, and so increased the guilt of Israel.
verses.kjv: And Ezra the priest stood up, and said unto them, Ye have transgressed, and have taken strange wives, to increase the trespass of Israel.
verses.bsb: Then Ezra the priest stood up and said to them, “You have been unfaithful by marrying foreign women, adding to the guilt of Israel.

verses.esv: Now then make confession to the LORD, the God of your fathers and do his will. Separate yourselves from the peoples of the land and from the foreign wives.”
verses.kjv: Now therefore make confession unto the Lord God of your fathers, and do his pleasure: and separate yourselves from the people of the land, and from the strange wives.
verses.bsb: Now, therefore, make a confession to the LORD, the God of your fathers, and do His will. Separate yourselves from the people of the land and from your foreign wives.”

verses.esv: Then all the assembly answered with a loud voice, “It is so; we must do as you have said.
verses.kjv: Then all the congregation answered and said with a loud voice, As thou hast said, so must we do.
verses.bsb: And the whole assembly responded in a loud voice: “Truly we must do as you say!

verses.esv: But the people are many, and it is a time of heavy rain; we cannot stand in the open. Nor is this a task for one day or for two, for we have greatly transgressed in this matter.
verses.kjv: But the people are many, and it is a time of much rain, and we are not able to stand without, neither is this a work of one day or two: for we are many that have transgressed in this thing.
verses.bsb: But there are many people here, and it is the rainy season. We are not able to stay out in the open. Nor is this the work of one or two days, for we have transgressed greatly in this matter.

verses.esv: Let our officials stand for the whole assembly. Let all in our cities who have taken foreign wives come at appointed times, and with them the elders and judges of every city, until the fierce wrath of our God over this matter is turned away from us.”
verses.kjv: Let now our rulers of all the congregation stand, and let all them which have taken strange wives in our cities come at appointed times, and with them the elders of every city, and the judges thereof, until the fierce wrath of our God for this matter be turned from us.
verses.bsb: Let our leaders represent the whole assembly. Then let everyone in our towns who has married a foreign woman come at an appointed time, together with the elders and judges of each town, until the fierce anger of our God in this matter is turned away from us.”

verses.esv: Only Jonathan the son of Asahel and Jahzeiah the son of Tikvah opposed this, and Meshullam and Shabbethai the Levite supported them.
verses.kjv: ¶ Only Jonathan the son of Asahel and Jahaziah the son of Tikvah were employed about this matter: and Meshullam and Shabbethai the Levite helped them.
verses.bsb: (Only Jonathan son of Asahel and Jahzeiah son of Tikvah, supported by Meshullam and Shabbethai the Levite, opposed this plan.)

verses.esv: Then the returned exiles did so. Ezra the priest selected men, heads of fathers' houses, according to their fathers' houses, each of them designated by name. On the first day of the tenth month they sat down to examine the matter;
verses.kjv: And the children of the captivity did so. And Ezra the priest, with certain chief of the fathers, after the house of their fathers, and all of them by their names, were separated, and sat down in the first day of the tenth month to examine the matter.
verses.bsb: So the exiles did as proposed. Ezra the priest selected men who were family heads, each of them identified by name, to represent their families. On the first day of the tenth month they launched the investigation,

verses.esv: and by the first day of the first month they had come to the end of all the men who had married foreign women.
verses.kjv: And they made an end with all the men that had taken strange wives by the first day of the first month.
verses.bsb: and by the first day of the first month they had dealt with all the men who had married foreign women.

verses.esv: Now there were found some of the sons of the priests who had married foreign women: Maaseiah, Eliezer, Jarib, and Gedaliah, some of the sons of Jeshua the son of Jozadak and his brothers.
verses.kjv: ¶ And among the sons of the priests there were found that had taken strange wives: namely, of the sons of Jeshua the son of Jozadak, and his brethren; Maaseiah, and Eliezer, and Jarib, and Gedaliah.
verses.bsb: Among the descendants of the priests who had married foreign women were found these descendants of Jeshua son of Jozadak and his brothers:

verses.esv: They pledged themselves to put away their wives, and their guilt offering was a ram of the flock for their guilt.
verses.kjv: And they gave their hands that they would put away their wives; and being guilty, they offered a ram of the flock for their trespass.
verses.bsb: Maaseiah, Eliezer, Jarib, and Gedaliah. They pledged to send their wives away, and for their guilt they presented a ram from the flock as a guilt offering.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Immer: Hanani and Zebadiah.
verses.kjv: And of the sons of Immer; Hanani, and Zebadiah.
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Immer:  Hanani and Zebadiah.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Harim: Maaseiah, Elijah, Shemaiah, Jehiel, and Uzziah.
verses.kjv: And of the sons of Harim; Maaseiah, and Elijah, and Shemaiah, and Jehiel, and Uzziah.
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Harim:  Maaseiah, Elijah, Shemaiah, Jehiel, and Uzziah.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Pashhur: Elioenai, Maaseiah, Ishmael, Nethanel, Jozabad, and Elasah.
verses.kjv: And of the sons of Pashur; Elioenai, Maaseiah, Ishmael, Nethaneel, Jozabad, and Elasah.
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Pashhur:  Elioenai, Maaseiah, Ishmael, Nethanel, Jozabad, and Elasah.

verses.esv: Of the Levites: Jozabad, Shimei, Kelaiah (that is, Kelita), Pethahiah, Judah, and Eliezer.
verses.kjv: Also of the Levites; Jozabad, and Shimei, and Kelaiah, (the same is Kelita,) Pethahiah, Judah, and Eliezer.
verses.bsb: Among the Levites:  Jozabad, Shimei, Kelaiah (that is, Kelita), Pethahiah, Judah, and Eliezer.

verses.esv: Of the singers: Eliashib. Of the gatekeepers: Shallum, Telem, and Uri.
verses.kjv: Of the singers also; Eliashib: and of the porters; Shallum, and Telem, and Uri.
verses.bsb: From the singers:  Eliashib.   From the gatekeepers:  Shallum, Telem, and Uri.

verses.esv: And of Israel: of the sons of Parosh: Ramiah, Izziah, Malchijah, Mijamin, Eleazar, Hashabiah, and Benaiah.
verses.kjv: Moreover of Israel: of the sons of Parosh; Ramiah, and Jeziah, and Malchiah, and Miamin, and Eleazar, and Malchijah, and Benaiah.
verses.bsb: And among the other Israelites, from the descendants of Parosh:  Ramiah, Izziah, Malchijah, Mijamin, Eleazar, Malchijah, and Benaiah.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Elam: Mattaniah, Zechariah, Jehiel, Abdi, Jeremoth, and Elijah.
verses.kjv: And of the sons of Elam; Mattaniah, Zechariah, and Jehiel, and Abdi, and Jeremoth, and Eliah.
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Elam:  Mattaniah, Zechariah, Jehiel, Abdi, Jeremoth, and Elijah.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Zattu: Elioenai, Eliashib, Mattaniah, Jeremoth, Zabad, and Aziza.
verses.kjv: And of the sons of Zattu; Elioenai, Eliashib, Mattaniah, and Jeremoth, and Zabad, and Aziza.
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Zattu:  Elioenai, Eliashib, Mattaniah, Jeremoth, Zabad, and Aziza.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Bebai were Jehohanan, Hananiah, Zabbai, and Athlai.
verses.kjv: Of the sons also of Bebai; Jehohanan, Hananiah, Zabbai, and Athlai.
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Bebai:  Jehohanan, Hananiah, Zabbai, and Athlai.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Bani were Meshullam, Malluch, Adaiah, Jashub, Sheal, and Jeremoth.
verses.kjv: And of the sons of Bani; Meshullam, Malluch, and Adaiah, Jashub, and Sheal, and Ramoth.
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Bani:  Meshullam, Malluch, Adaiah, Jashub, Sheal, and Jeremoth.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Pahath-moab: Adna, Chelal, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattaniah, Bezalel, Binnui, and Manasseh.
verses.kjv: And of the sons of Pahath-moab; Adna, and Chelal, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattaniah, Bezaleel, and Binnui, and Manasseh.
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Pahath-moab:  Adna, Chelal, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattaniah, Bezalel, Binnui, and Manasseh.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Harim: Eliezer, Isshijah, Malchijah, Shemaiah, Shimeon,
verses.kjv: And of the sons of Harim; Eliezer, Ishijah, Malchiah, Shemaiah, Shimeon,
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Harim:

verses.esv: Benjamin, Malluch, and Shemariah.
verses.kjv: Benjamin, Malluch, and Shemariah.
verses.bsb: Eliezer, Isshijah, Malchijah, Shemaiah, Shimeon, Benjamin, Malluch, and Shemariah.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Hashum: Mattenai, Mattattah, Zabad, Eliphelet, Jeremai, Manasseh, and Shimei.
verses.kjv: Of the sons of Hashum; Mattenai, Mattathah, Zabad, Eliphelet, Jeremai, Manasseh, and Shimei.
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Hashum:  Mattenai, Mattattah, Zabad, Eliphelet, Jeremai, Manasseh, and Shimei.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Bani: Maadai, Amram, Uel,
verses.kjv: Of the sons of Bani; Maadai, Amram, and Uel,
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Bani:

verses.esv: Benaiah, Bedeiah, Cheluhi,
verses.kjv: Benaiah, Bedeiah, Chelluh,
verses.bsb: Maadai, Amram, Uel, Benaiah, Bedeiah, Cheluhi,

verses.esv: Vaniah, Meremoth, Eliashib,
verses.kjv: Vaniah, Meremoth, Eliashib,
verses.bsb: Vaniah, Meremoth, Eliashib,

verses.esv: Mattaniah, Mattenai, Jaasu.
verses.kjv: Mattaniah, Mattenai, and Jaasau,
verses.bsb: Mattaniah, Mattenai, and Jaasu.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Binnui: Shimei,
verses.kjv: And Bani, and Binnui, Shimei,
verses.bsb: From the descendants of Binnui:

verses.esv: Shelemiah, Nathan, Adaiah,
verses.kjv: And Shelemiah, and Nathan, and Adaiah,
verses.bsb: Shimei, Shelemiah, Nathan, Adaiah,

verses.esv: Machnadebai, Shashai, Sharai,
verses.kjv: Machnadebai, Shashai, Sharai,
verses.bsb: Machnadebai, Shashai, Sharai,

verses.esv: Azarel, Shelemiah, Shemariah,
verses.kjv: Azareel, and Shelemiah, Shemariah,
verses.bsb: Azarel, Shelemiah, Shemariah,

verses.esv: Shallum, Amariah, and Joseph.
verses.kjv: Shallum, Amariah, and Joseph.
verses.bsb: Shallum, Amariah, and Joseph.

verses.esv: Of the sons of Nebo: Jeiel, Mattithiah, Zabad, Zebina, Jaddai, Joel, and Benaiah.
verses.kjv: Of the sons of Nebo; Jeiel, Mattithiah, Zabad, Zebina, Jadau, and Joel, Benaiah.
verses.bsb: And from the descendants of Nebo:  Jeiel, Mattithiah, Zabad, Zebina, Jaddai, Joel, and Benaiah.

verses.esv: All these had married foreign women, and some of the women had even borne children.
verses.kjv: All these had taken strange wives: and some of them had wives by whom they had children. THE
verses.bsb: All these men had married foreign women, and some of them had children by these wives.
